CHIU, DAVID, PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO LEE, EDWIN M MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HEARING SET FOR MAY-27-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS EDWIN M. LEE, MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DAVID CHIU, PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND REAL PARTY IN INTEREST RICHARD S.E. JOHNS AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING OF DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DEFENDANTS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN QUO WARRANTO TO BE AN INADEQUATE ALTERNATIVE REMEDY. AS SUCH A WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS AN INAPPROPRIATE REMEDY PER CCP SECTION 1086. RESPONDENT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: MITCH LYON, CSR #6840

calendar.matters:
AMENDED DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RESPONDENTS EDWIN M. LEE, MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DAVID CHIU, PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND REAL PARTY IN INTEREST RICHARD S.E. JOHNS AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING OF DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

APPEARANCES: Arthur D. Levy, Esq. 702-4550 for petitioners & opposing parties; Andrew Shen, Deputy City Atty 554-4780 for respondents & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - DEFENDANTS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN QUO WARRANTO TO BE AN INADEQUATE ALTERNATIVE REMEDY. AS SUCH A WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS AN INAPPROPRIATE REMEDY PER CCP SECTION 1086. Respondent to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR #6840

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to June 28, 2011 on the court's own motion. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
